providerDetectionSource: DATABASE_URL hostname class reported as starting with dpg; full DATABASE_URL was not printed or stored

blocker.exactBlocker: Cannot run pg_dump from this environment because DATABASE_URL is not available to the shell/.env and pg_dump is not installed locally.

requiredManualAction: In Render, locate the database connection string without copying it into logs.
Prefer Render provider-native Recovery/Export if the Postgres service becomes visible in the Render dashboard.
If no native export is visible, run pg_dump from a machine with PostgreSQL client tools installed using DATABASE_URL as an environment variable, never echoing the URL.
Save the backup as artifacts/backup-rollback-v1.0.127/fima-db-backup-before-v1.0.127.sql or .sql.gz before deploy/runtime jobs.
safePgDumpCommandPattern: Set DATABASE_URL in the process environment without printing it.
Run pg_dump with --dbname from the DATABASE_URL environment variable.
Write output to artifacts/backup-rollback-v1.0.127/fima-db-backup-before-v1.0.127.sql or pipe to gzip if available.
rollbackNotes: Do not run deploy, admin runtime jobs, Stripe product setup, webhook replay, checkout verification, or license/gift mutation until a database backup/export exists.
If runtime jobs later mutate data incorrectly, restore from the pre-runtime database backup or apply targeted compensating changes using audit evidence.
Keep old Stripe products/prices and old license rows intact; rollback should restore env/database state rather than deleting historical records.
